human_verification.notes: Reviewed aviation-specific compliance boundaries, dispatch and load evidence, air waybill and chain-of-custody handling, cargo screening, customs documentation, dangerous-goods controls, route disruption, equipment and maintenance status, domain research coverage, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, regulator-facing, dispatch, customs, safety, security, dangerous-goods, maintenance, and formal signoff authority.

review_notes: All required issue scenario families and the transportation-and-logistics research contract families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus transportation-and-logistics are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to aviation compliance mapping, evidence handling, movement documentation checks, review preparation, remediation routing, and escalation support.

Authority sources cover FAA air-carrier, airport, SMS, maintenance, and electronic-record expectations; TSA cargo screening and security-program boundaries; CBP air-commerce and manifest documentation; PHMSA/DOT dangerous-goods carriage by aircraft; and IATA cargo handling terminology.

Scenario set covers missing evidence, chain-of-custody breaks, route and schedule disruption, conflicting public or tenant sources, legal or formal signoff requests, dispatch, customs, safety and clearance authority refusal, equipment and maintenance status gaps, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant-specific adaptation.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific security programs, customs instructions, jurisdiction, airport, route, IATA manual detail, service-level, and approval differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
